growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron's June 24 Q3 FY26 results and today's KeyBanc upgrade point to persistent HBM and DRAM shortages through 2027, with HBM prices expected to more than double and Micron's domestic capex push reinforcing supply discipline.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is converting AI memory scarcity into record earnings, supply agreements, and still-cheap forward valuation.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Meta has a July 29 earnings catalyst with rising revenue expectations, and the stock has already been sensitive to capex and AI monetization headlines, so the next print can move it sharply either way.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Meta pairs exceptional ad profitability with a live AI monetization catalyst and still trades at a reasonable forward multiple for its growth rate.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Reuters reported that L3Harris won a $955 million contract to supply 18 satellites for the Golden Dome missile shield, giving the company a direct backlog boost from U.S. missile-defense spending.
growth_picks.analyst_take: L3Harris has fresh Golden Dome satellite awards, record backlog momentum, and a near-term earnings catalyst at a valuation still below defense peers with cleaner growth visibility.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Intuit is still roughly 65% below its 52-week high despite intact profitability, and the market is now staring at an August 20, 2026 earnings report plus continued buybacks after management called the stock misaligned with fundamentals; the bear case is that AI fears keep the multiple depressed longer than expected.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Intuit is a high-margin software franchise priced like the AI bear case is already right, while fundamentals and buybacks remain intact.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Autodesk is about 36% below its 52-week high while still growing profitably, and it has an August 27, 2026 earnings catalyst plus a strong analyst target stack that implies meaningful re-rating room; the bear case is that software multiples keep compressing if macro risk stays elevated.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Autodesk combines drawdown, strong margins, raised guidance, and attractive forward valuation for a cleaner software value setup.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's tightening rare-earth export controls and retaliatory export restrictions on U.S. firms explicitly hit MP Materials, reinforcing the scarcity premium on non-China supply and increasing the strategic value of domestic rare-earth processing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP Materials is a strategic rare-earth supply-chain winner with DoD and Apple-backed demand signals, but negative FCF and commodity sensitivity keep it below strong-buy status.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Oracle's June 10 Q4/FY26 print showed cloud infrastructure revenue up 93% and RPO at $638 billion, and the July 9 S&P downgrade plus OpenAI model rollout keeps the AI-cloud backlog and funding needs front and center.
growth_picks.analyst_take: OCI backlog and AI demand create material upside, but debt-funded buildout and OpenAI concentration keep this below strong-buy quality.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The stock has sold off sharply after a fiscal Q3 revenue-growth cut and is now roughly 40% below its 2026 highs, but Morningstar still treats Accenture as a wide-moat business with durable AI-booking momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Accenture offers quality free cash flow at a reset multiple, but consulting demand and federal weakness cap conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Ross Bhappu bought 74,000 shares for about $968K on July 7, and the company is simultaneously pushing a $1.9B VAC acquisition plus July rare-earth circuit modifications, giving the insider buy real operational backing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy Fuels has a clean insider-buy signal plus uranium and rare-earth catalysts, but negative cash flow keeps sizing conservative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Ciena's June 4 Q2 FY26 results grew revenue 40% and raised FY26 guidance 32% at the midpoint, while its June 17 and June 23 Blue Planet AI-agent announcements show optical and network-automation demand broadening beyond transport optics.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Ciena has real AI optical demand and raised guidance, with enough pullback from highs to justify a measured buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Zillow has been cut in half from its 52-week high and is still only about 10% above the recent low, yet it remains profitable and has an August 5, 2026 earnings catalyst with consensus upside near 98%; the bear case is that housing activity stays frozen by rates and affordability.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Zillow is a contrarian housing-recovery setup with deep drawdown, cheap forward earnings, and large target upside.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Boot Barn has pulled back from its highs but still screens as undervalued, with GuruFocus flagging it as down for the year while MarketWatch shows the stock holding well above recent lows and Zacks still calling it a long-term growth name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Boot Barn has real same-store growth and store expansion momentum at a reasonable forward multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Lowe's looks like a quality large-cap pullback, with GuruFocus estimating intrinsic value above the current price and MarketWatch highlighting the stock as undervalued after a recent slide.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lowe's is a quality pullback with strong cash generation and housing-cycle optionality, though near-term comps are muted.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Alphabet has a clear binary event on July 22 earnings, with analysts expecting year-over-year EPS and revenue growth and the stock still sitting below its recent high.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Alphabet is a high-quality AI/cloud compounder with a near-term Q2 earnings catalyst, but rising capex and antitrust risk keep this from being a table-pounder.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Axios reported that the Pentagon awarded Lockheed Martin an initial $86 million Joint Laser Weapon System contract, extending its role in high-energy air and missile defense as the U.S. budget shifts toward directed-energy systems.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed is a defense value setup with a July earnings reset and directed-energy/missile-defense catalysts, but Q1 weakness argues against overconfidence.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Peter R. Matt purchased 8,230 shares on 2026-07-10 at $61.30 for $504,499, lifting his holding to 181,522 shares and marking a rare six-figure open-market buy from the top executive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Commercial Metals offers a cheap cyclical value setup reinforced by a rare CEO open-market purchase and improving Q3 profitability.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Figma is one of the most heavily shorted large-cap names right now with 44.44% short interest and 4.07 days to cover, while Bank of America is still modeling 35.6% revenue growth in 2026 and 23% in 2027, making it a live squeeze candidate if the post-IPO momentum holds.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Figma combines accelerating revenue growth, high net retention, and heavy short interest, creating a credible squeeze-and-growth setup after an 80%+ drawdown.

catalysts_general: Discovery: EquipmentShare raised full-year 2026 guidance and authorized a new $500M repurchase program through December 31, 2028, which is meaningful because the company also lifted revenue and EBITDA outlooks and had been trading with a market cap above $4B.
growth_picks.analyst_take: EquipmentShare has a credible rerating catalyst from raised 2026 guidance and a large buyback, but leverage and negative FCF make position sizing important.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv opened a new Johor, Malaysia facility on July 1 to expand power, cooling, and integrated infrastructure capacity for AI and high-density computing across Asia, which directly addresses the bottleneck that hyperscale AI data centers are facing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv remains a direct AI data-center infrastructure beneficiary with backlog visibility, but valuation and beta argue for disciplined entries only.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PayPal is still a large profitable franchise, but it is roughly 40% below its 52-week high and only about 23% above the low; Q2 earnings are due on July 28, 2026, which gives the stock a near-term reset point if margins and buybacks hold up, though competition and TPV deceleration remain the main bear cases.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is a beaten-down FCF value stock with a July earnings reset and a recent CFO purchase, though competition and weak target upside keep conviction moderate.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Zoetis is more than 50% below its 52-week high after a rough run of pet-health sentiment, yet it remains a wide-moat animal-health leader with a resilient recurring-demand profile.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Zoetis is a high-margin animal-health franchise priced like a broken stock, but Librela weakness and litigation make this a turnaround value call rather than a clean compounder.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IBM just suffered a record one-day collapse after a surprise Q2 miss, but it is still profitable, roughly 35% below its 52-week high, and has final Q2 results due on July 22, 2026; the bear case is that Z-system weakness and delayed deals keep pressuring near-term numbers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IBM is cheaper after the earnings warning, but the miss exposed execution and AI-spend displacement risk that need final Q2 confirmation.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Bloom Energy has a near-term earnings catalyst with estimates implying sharp revenue and EPS acceleration, which makes it one of the cleaner asymmetry names in the current market.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Bloom has a real AI power catalyst, but valuation, leverage, volatility, and a fresh short-seller dispute make the entry undisciplined.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Exxon remains one of the clearest energy rotation beneficiaries, with Morningstar pointing to its contribution to sector leadership in 2026 and higher fair value estimates after updated guidance.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Exxon is a high-quality energy rotation beneficiary, but current upside is only moderate and profits may be near a cyclical peak.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Nike has been sold down hard, sitting about 46.5% below its 52-week high and just 7.1% above the low; the next earnings date is September 29, 2026, so this is a classic capitulation setup if management can show inventory and demand stabilization, but a slow recovery in wholesale and China would keep the bear case alive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nike is washed out enough to watch, but the turnaround still lacks hard evidence of sustained China, wholesale, and inventory recovery.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista's June 9 launch of its 1.6T 7060XE7 AI fabric portfolio and the July 7 IR update show it is shipping the next switch generation for rack-scale AI clusters, directly tied to Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle buildouts.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Arista is a premier AI networking compounder, but the stock is too close to highs and too close to consensus fair value to chase.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Workday has come off a much higher range, yet both GuruFocus and MarketWatch still frame it as undervalued after a pullback, which makes it a cleaner quality-dip setup than a broken story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Workday is cheaper than usual and has AI-agent traction, but insider selling and limited target upside argue for patience.

catalysts_general: Discovery: AJG has dropped nearly 20% from its 52-week high while still trading below GuruFocus fair value, which creates a rare pullback in a historically steady insurance broker.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Gallagher is a durable insurance broker, but the pullback is not deep enough and upside is modest at the current price.

catalysts_general: Discovery: A VF board member bought 30,000 shares for about $515K, and the stock still trades well below its prior highs, which fits the classic insider-confidence setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: VF's turnaround is finally showing signs of life, but high leverage and limited target upside keep it below buy status.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Caterpillar is a direct beneficiary of the 2026 rotation into industrials, and Morningstar explicitly tied it to AI data-center buildout demand while the stock remains below its recent high.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Caterpillar has real AI-data-center and power-generation demand, but the stock already prices in too much of the upside after a major run.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The NATO summit in Ankara moved Raytheon's AMRAAM program into European co-production for the first time, which should support backlog growth and de-risk missile supply at a moment when allies are racing to replenish air-defense inventories.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has strong NATO missile-demand tailwinds, but the stock is not cheap enough after its run to absorb execution or engine-program risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Sempra’s ECA LNG Phase 1 loaded its first cargo from Mexico’s Pacific coast, creating a shorter Asia route that avoids the Strait of Hormuz and improves the strategic value of Sempra’s LNG export infrastructure during Middle East disruptions.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Sempra's Pacific LNG milestone is strategically useful, but the utility valuation and negative free cash flow leave limited upside at current prices.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Investor's Business Daily flagged International Seaways as a tanker-stock momentum name after Trump reversed a proposed Strait of Hormuz fee, while AP and WSJ coverage shows tanker attacks and route risk are keeping freight rates elevated.
growth_picks.analyst_take: International Seaways is benefiting from elevated tanker rates and geopolitical freight disruption, but the stock is already near its high and the cycle can reverse quickly.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Three insiders bought on the same date: EVP Douglas A. Virtue bought 8,083 shares at $5.997 (~$48.5K), CEO Robert A. Virtue bought 4,300 shares at $6.22 (~$26.7K), and director Robert R. Lind bought 500 shares at $6.07 (~$3.0K), a tight open-market cluster totaling about $78K.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Virco has a constructive insider-buying cluster and cheap headline valuation, but declining revenue and weak margins make the turnaround too unproven.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Anastasios Arima bought 138,720 ordinary shares on 2026-07-10 at A$3.5844 for about A$497,228, increasing his direct stake to 12,455,502 shares and signaling conviction in the advanced-materials story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IperionX has strong insider sponsorship and a strategic U.S. titanium story, but dilution and lack of proven profitability keep it speculative.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Applied Materials has a direct AI wafer-fab catalyst: it introduced new DRAM and advanced-packaging systems on June 25, and Meta's July 9 Iris chip plan pushed WFE names higher, reinforcing AMAT as a levered beneficiary of leading-edge and memory capex.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Applied Materials is a real AI wafer-fab beneficiary, but current valuation and near-flat target upside make it hard to recommend chasing.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Palantir continues to show real enterprise AI monetization: its Q1 2026 update showed U.S. commercial revenue up 133% and TCV up 45%, and its July release cycle added pro-code Foundry agents, MCP support, and new customer expansion in Mexico and insurance.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Palantir's AI growth is excellent, but the valuation is too extreme and insider selling/political scrutiny cap risk-adjusted upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Seagate is one of the clearest AI-storage beneficiaries right now: Wells Fargo raised its target and the stock still trades well below its June high ahead of late-July earnings.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Seagate has powerful AI storage demand and analyst support, but after a huge rerating the balance-sheet and valuation risk are too high for a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CrowdStrike's June 3 Q1 FY27 report delivered record net new ARR of $256 million and 24% ARR growth to $5.51 billion, while OpenAI and Anthropic partnerships plus the July 14 cybersecurity rally keep AI security spending hot.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CrowdStrike remains an excellent operator, but the stock is priced for perfection at a 52-week high with analyst targets below market.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO John F. Barry bought more than 1.0 million shares in open-market trades, a very large insider signal for a stock already near the bottom of its range.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Large CEO buying is not enough to offset deteriorating revenue, weak institutional support, and an analyst target below the stock.
